sn18.2:1.1:
… "What do you think, Rahula, are forms …
… sounds … odours … tastes … tactile objects … mental phenomena permanent or impermanent?" –"Impermanent, venerable sir." …
sn18.2:1.2: "Seeing thus, Rahula, the instructed noble disciple experiences revulsion towards forms … revulsion towards mental phenomena. Experiencing revulsion, he becomes dispassionate … . He understands: ‘… there is no more for this state of being.’"
